intro: Isodeca flaccida, also known as Mexican Prickly Poppy, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to Mexico, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Isodeca flaccida include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, such as isodeacine and deacetylepinephrine, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, often combined with other plants to enhance its medicinal properties.
